---
title: "DM_R025: Type Conversion Between R and DuckDB"
subtitle: "Establish explicit type mappings and conversion rules between R and DuckDB data types to ensure data integrity during transfer"
chapter: "CH02"
category: "rule"
number: "DM_R025"
date-created: "2025-08-27"
date-modified: "2025-08-27"
author: "Claude (Principle Revisor)"
type: "rule"
law: "Data Management Law"
article:
  number: 25
  title: "R-DuckDB Type Conversion Rules"
related_to:
  - "DM_R024: List Column Handling Rule"
  - "DM_R023: Universal DBI Approach"
  - "MP063: Data Processing Trinity"
  - "DM_R001: Data Frame Creation Strategy"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 25: R-DuckDB Type Conversion Rules {#overview}

**Data Management Law Article 25 (Type Conversion Rules)**

This rule establishes the canonical type mappings between R and DuckDB, ensuring consistent and predictable data transfer.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
All data transfers between R and DuckDB MUST follow the established type mapping table. Complex types require explicit conversion before transfer.
:::

# Section 1: Type Mapping Table {#type-mapping}

## Subsection 1: Basic Type Mappings {#basic-types}

| R Type | DuckDB Type | Notes | Conversion Required |
|--------|-------------|-------|---------------------|
| `logical` | `BOOLEAN` | Direct mapping | No |
| `integer` | `INTEGER` | 32-bit signed | No |
| `numeric`/`double` | `DOUBLE` | 64-bit float | No |
| `character` | `VARCHAR` | Variable-length string | No |
| `Date` | `DATE` | Calendar date | No |
| `POSIXct` | `TIMESTAMP` | Date + time | No |
| `POSIXlt` | `TIMESTAMP` | Requires conversion | Yes - convert to POSIXct |
| `factor` | `VARCHAR` | Loses factor levels | Yes - as.character() |
| `raw` | `BLOB` | Binary data | No |

## Subsection 2: Complex Type Mappings {#complex-types}

| R Type | DuckDB Type | Conversion Strategy | Notes |
|--------|-------------|---------------------|--------|
| `list` | Not supported | Convert to JSON VARCHAR | See DM_R024 |
| `data.frame` | Not supported | Flatten or nest as JSON | Depends on use case |
| `matrix` | Multiple columns | Separate columns per dimension | Reshape first |
| `array` | Not supported | Flatten or serialize | Context-dependent |
| `S3/S4 objects` | Not supported | Extract data slots | Custom conversion |
| `NULL` | `NULL` | Direct mapping | No conversion |
| `NA` (logical) | `NULL` | Direct mapping | No conversion |
| `NA_integer_` | `NULL` | Direct mapping | No conversion |
| `NA_real_` | `NULL` | Direct mapping | No conversion |
| `NA_character_` | `NULL` | Direct mapping | No conversion |

# Section 2: Conversion Functions {#conversion-functions}

## Subsection 1: Pre-Write Conversion {#pre-write}

Standard function to prepare R data for DuckDB:

```r
#' Convert R data frame for DuckDB compatibility
#' @param df Data frame to convert
#' @return Data frame with DuckDB-compatible types
convert_r_to_duckdb <- function(df) {
  df_converted <- df
  
  for (col in names(df_converted)) {
    col_data <- df_converted[[col]]
    
    # Handle factors
    if (is.factor(col_data)) {
      df_converted[[col]] <- as.character(col_data)
      message(sprintf("Converted factor column '%s' to character", col))
    }
    
    # Handle POSIXlt
    else if (inherits(col_data, "POSIXlt")) {
      df_converted[[col]] <- as.POSIXct(col_data)
      message(sprintf("Converted POSIXlt column '%s' to POSIXct", col))
    }
    
    # Handle lists (see DM_R024)
    else if (is.list(col_data) && !is.data.frame(col_data)) {
      df_converted[[paste0(col, "_json")]] <- sapply(col_data, 
                                                      jsonlite::toJSON, 
                                                      auto_unbox = TRUE)
      df_converted[[col]] <- NULL
      message(sprintf("Converted list column '%s' to JSON", col))
    }
    
    # Handle matrices
    else if (is.matrix(col_data)) {
      # Flatten matrix to separate columns
      for (i in 1:ncol(col_data)) {
        df_converted[[paste0(col, "_", i)]] <- col_data[, i]
      }
      df_converted[[col]] <- NULL
      message(sprintf("Flattened matrix column '%s' to %d columns", 
                      col, ncol(col_data)))
    }
  }
  
  return(df_converted)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Post-Read Conversion {#post-read}

Convert DuckDB results back to expected R types:

```r
#' Convert DuckDB query results to R types
#' @param df Data frame from DuckDB query
#' @param schema Optional schema specification
#' @return Data frame with appropriate R types
convert_duckdb_to_r <- function(df, schema = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(schema)) {
    for (col in names(schema)) {
      if (col %in% names(df)) {
        target_type <- schema[[col]]
        
        # Restore factors
        if (target_type == "factor" && is.character(df[[col]])) {
          df[[col]] <- as.factor(df[[col]])
        }
        
        # Parse JSON columns back to lists
        else if (target_type == "list" && is.character(df[[col]])) {
          df[[col]] <- lapply(df[[col]], jsonlite::fromJSON)
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(df)
}
```

# Section 3: Special Cases {#special-cases}

## Subsection 1: Integer vs Numeric {#integer-numeric}

DuckDB distinguishes between INTEGER and DOUBLE, while R often promotes to numeric:

```r
# R may silently promote integers
df$count <- 1:10          # Creates integer
df$count <- df$count + 0.5  # Promotes to numeric

# Explicit control for DuckDB
df$count <- as.integer(df$count)  # Ensure INTEGER type
```

## Subsection 2: Large Numbers {#large-numbers}

DuckDB supports BIGINT and HUGEINT, R has limitations:

```r
# R's integer maximum: 2^31 - 1 = 2,147,483,647
# Values larger than this become numeric (double)

# For large IDs, use numeric in R, BIGINT in DuckDB
df$large_id <- as.numeric("9223372036854775807")  # Max BIGINT

# Check for integer overflow
check_integer_overflow <- function(x) {
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    max_val <- max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (max_val > .Machine$integer.max) {
      warning("Values exceed R integer range, using numeric")
      return(TRUE)
    }
  }
  return(FALSE)
}
```

## Subsection 3: Time Zones {#timezones}

Handle timezone-aware timestamps carefully:

```r
# DuckDB TIMESTAMP vs TIMESTAMPTZ
# R POSIXct can have timezone attributes

# Ensure consistent timezone handling
df$timestamp <- as.POSIXct(df$timestamp, tz = "UTC")

# For TIMESTAMPTZ in DuckDB
df$timestamp_tz <- format(df$timestamp, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
```

# Section 4: Validation Functions {#validation}

## Subsection 1: Type Compatibility Check {#compatibility-check}

```r
#' Check if data frame is DuckDB-compatible
#' @param df Data frame to check
#' @return List with compatibility status and issues
check_duckdb_compatibility <- function(df) {
  issues <- list()
  
  for (col in names(df)) {
    col_class <- class(df[[col]])[1]
    
    # Check for problematic types
    if (col_class == "list") {
      issues[[length(issues) + 1]] <- sprintf(
        "Column '%s' is a list - needs JSON conversion", col)
    }
    else if (col_class == "factor") {
      issues[[length(issues) + 1]] <- sprintf(
        "Column '%s' is a factor - will be converted to character", col)
    }
    else if (col_class == "POSIXlt") {
      issues[[length(issues) + 1]] <- sprintf(
        "Column '%s' is POSIXlt - needs conversion to POSIXct", col)
    }
    else if (col_class %in% c("matrix", "array")) {
      issues[[length(issues) + 1]] <- sprintf(
        "Column '%s' is %s - needs flattening", col, col_class)
    }
  }
  
  return(list(
    compatible = length(issues) == 0,
    issues = issues
  ))
}
```

## Subsection 2: Automatic Type Fixing {#auto-fix}

```r
#' Automatically fix type compatibility issues
#' @param df Data frame to fix
#' @param verbose Print conversion messages
#' @return Fixed data frame
fix_for_duckdb <- function(df, verbose = TRUE) {
  check_result <- check_duckdb_compatibility(df)
  
  if (check_result$compatible) {
    if (verbose) message("Data frame is already DuckDB-compatible")
    return(df)
  }
  
  if (verbose) {
    message("Fixing compatibility issues:")
    for (issue in check_result$issues) {
      message("  - ", issue)
    }
  }
  
  return(convert_r_to_duckdb(df))
}
```

# Section 5: Performance Implications {#performance}

## Subsection 1: Type Conversion Overhead {#conversion-overhead}

Different conversions have different costs:

| Conversion | Performance Impact | Recommendation |
|------------|-------------------|----------------|
| factor → character | Low | Always safe |
| POSIXlt → POSIXct | Low | Always convert |
| list → JSON | Medium-High | Consider alternatives |
| matrix → columns | Medium | Depends on size |
| Large numeric → BIGINT | Low | Automatic |

## Subsection 2: Memory Considerations {#memory}

```r
# Memory usage comparison
compare_memory_usage <- function(df) {
  original_size <- object.size(df)
  converted_df <- convert_r_to_duckdb(df)
  converted_size <- object.size(converted_df)
  
  list(
    original = format(original_size, units = "MB"),
    converted = format(converted_size, units = "MB"),
    ratio = as.numeric(converted_size) / as.numeric(original_size)
  )
}
```

# Section 6: Integration with Existing Rules {#integration}

This rule complements:

- **DM_R024**: Specific handling for list columns
- **DM_R023**: Universal DBI approach
- **DM_R001**: Data frame creation strategy

Use in conjunction with these rules for complete data management.

# Section 7: Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

When transferring data between R and DuckDB:

1. ☐ Run `check_duckdb_compatibility()` on data frame
2. ☐ Apply `fix_for_duckdb()` if issues found
3. ☐ Document any JSON columns created
4. ☐ Verify numeric precision requirements
5. ☐ Check date/time timezone handling
6. ☐ Test with sample data before full transfer
7. ☐ Monitor performance for large datasets

---

*Based on: DuckDB R client documentation and empirical testing*
*Essential for: All R-DuckDB data transfers, ETL pipelines, data persistence*
